name: Radxa ROCK 5B+
description: RK3588 refresh with M.2 and storage upgrades

release-date.comment: Radxa announced general availability around 2024-07-23/24 (Liliputing pickup 2024-07-23, CNX-Software 2024-07-27).

status.comment: Currently the active flagship of the ROCK 5 line. Verified 2026-04-26: Radxa lists it on the product page; Arace shows the listing live (all SKUs flagged Sold Out at the time of check, indicating intermittent supply rather than EOL).

open-source-hardware.comment: Radxa publishes schematic PDFs, 2D DXF outlines, and 3D STP models for the ROCK 5B family but no PCB layout source / Gerber / BOM. Per RESEARCH.md, schematics-only does not qualify as OSHW.

country-of-origin.comment: Radxa is headquartered in Shenzhen, Guangdong.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: A76 cluster up to 2.4 GHz; A55 cluster up to 1.8 GHz (same SoC bin as the original 5B).

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G610 MC4 supports OpenGL ES 3.2 / OpenCL 2.2 / Vulkan 1.2 per Arm spec.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G610 supports Vulkan 1.2; mainline Panthor driver upstreamed in kernel 6.10 (2024) and shipping in Armbian's mainline track.

npu-performance-tops.comment: Vendor-quoted 6 TOPS @ INT8 (RK3588 NPU). Same silicon as the original 5B.

hw-video-encode.comment: Up to 8K@30 encode; AV1 encode is NOT supported on RK3588.

ram-max-gb.comment: Five RAM tiers offered: 4/8/16/24/32 GB. The 24 GB and 32 GB SKUs are exclusive to the 5B+; the original 5B tops out at 16 GB.

ram-type.comment: LPDDR5 — primary upgrade vs. the LPDDR4x on the original 5B.

emmc.comment: Onboard eMMC up to 128 GB (32/64/128 GB SKU options). Soldered down rather than the swappable connector used on the original 5B.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: Two M.2 M-key sockets, each PCIe 3.0 ×2 (vs. the single M.2 M-key PCIe 3.0 ×4 on the original 5B). Tradeoff: more drives, but each runs at half the lane width.

sata.comment: No native SATA port; storage attaches via the M.2 NVMe slots, USB, or the M.2 B-key cellular slot.

wifi.comment: Onboard Realtek RTL8852BE — Wi-Fi 6 (2×2 MIMO, 802.11ax) with two antenna connectors. Onboard wireless is a key 5B+ upgrade — the original 5B has no onboard Wi-Fi.

bluetooth.comment: Bluetooth 5.2 via the same RTL8852BE combo module.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: 2× USB 3.1 Gen 1 (5 Gbps) Type-A host ports.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB-C (USB 3.1 Gen 1) data port with DisplayPort alt-mode and OTG. A SECOND USB-C connector exists as power-only (USB-PD) and is not counted here per the attribute definition (data-capable only).

poe-support.comment: PoE pads broken out on the GPIO header for an external PoE HAT; not built into the Ethernet port.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 2× HDMI 2.1 outputs (Type-A): one up to 8K@60, one up to 4K@60. Plus a separate full-size HDMI INPUT (4K@60) — upgraded from the micro-HDMI input on the original 5B; not counted here.

displayport.comment: DisplayPort via USB-C alt-mode (up to 4K@60); no dedicated DP connector.

mipi-csi.comment: 2× 4-lane MIPI CSI (or 4× 2-lane), supporting up to 4 cameras — broader than the original 5B.

audio-jack.comment: 4-ring 3.5 mm TRRS jack with line-out and mic-in.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin header in Raspberry Pi 40-pin pinout, with I2C/SPI/UART/I2S/PWM.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pi-compatible 40-pin pinout, but PCB is 100×75 mm (not RPi standard) so HAT mechanical mounting and EEPROM auto-config are not RPi-spec compliant.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: Two M.2 M-key 2280 sockets for NVMe SSDs (PCIe 3.0 ×2 each) plus a new M.2 B-key (3042) socket with SIM slot for 4G LTE / 5G modems. The 5B's PCIe 3.0 ×4 single slot was split into two ×2 slots.

power-input.comment: Dedicated power-only USB-C port with USB-PD 2.0 (separate from the data-capable USB-C). Also accepts fixed 5–20V on the same port. ≥30 W PD supply recommended.

idle-power-w.comment: No reliable measured idle figure for the 5B+ specifically. Profile is broadly comparable to the original 5B (~1.7 W headless, ~5–8 W desktop) but onboard Wi-Fi 6 adds load and LPDDR5 idle differs from LPDDR4x. Marked null per RESEARCH.md guidance to avoid extrapolating from the 5B.

load-power-w.comment: No reliable measured 5B+-specific full-load figure. The same RK3588 SoC on the 5B draws ~10–15 W under stress; expect the 5B+ to land in the same range. Marked null to avoid copying a non-5B+ measurement.

cooling-required.comment: Same RK3588 thermal envelope as the original 5B; sustained load throttles on a passive heatsink. Radxa ships an active heatsink + fan accessory and the 5B+ adds a dedicated PWM fan header.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC battery connector retained from the 5B.

width-mm.comment: 100×75 mm — 3 mm wider than the original 5B's 100×72 mm Pico-ITX footprint, so 5B-designed cases are not guaranteed to fit.

form-factor-class.comment: 100×75 mm exceeds the 100×72 mm Pico-ITX standard by 3 mm. Per RESEARCH.md's strict form-factor classification, this is `custom` rather than `pico-itx`. Visually similar to Pico-ITX but mechanically incompatible with strict Pico-ITX cases.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Custom four-corner mounting pattern, not Raspberry Pi standard. Differs slightly from the original 5B due to the wider PCB.

weight-g.comment: Vendor does not publish a bare-board weight; not reported in major reviews. Marked null per RESEARCH.md guidance.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Armbian ships both a current/mainline (6.18) and vendor (6.1) track and tags the 5B+ as Platinum/Stable. As with other RK3588 boards, mainline boots and the major peripherals work (USB3, 2.5 GbE, Mali-G610 via Panthor, HDMI display) but hardware video decode (VDPU381), NPU, MIPI DSI, USB-PD negotiation, and the onboard RTL8852BE Wi-Fi/BT are still maturing as of kernel 6.18 (early 2026).

official-os-image.comment: Radxa publishes a Debian Bookworm KDE image (kernel 6.1) and Android 12 images (SD/eMMC and SPI-NVMe) specifically for the 5B+.

distros-with-images.comment: Vendor Debian image plus first-class Armbian support (Ubuntu 24.04 / Debian 13 / Ubuntu 26.04 with mainline + vendor kernels). DietPi/Manjaro/openFyde are reported to work but not enumerated as 5B+-specific images at the time of check.

long-term-support.comment: Radxa does not publish a formal support window for the 5B+. The ongoing RK3588 mainlining effort (Collabora) and active Armbian Platinum status suggest multi-year practical support, but it is not contractually stated.

android-support.comment: Official Android 12 image (SD/eMMC and SPI-NVMe variants).

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch base price (4 GB, no eMMC). Snapshot 2026-04-26: Arace lists base SKU at $75. LinuxGizmos quoted launch tier at $69.66 (4 GB) — the few-dollar drift reflects the difference between AliExpress promo and Arace retail.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Launch top tier (32 GB LPDDR5, no eMMC) at $198. The 16 GB and 24 GB tiers sit between $119 and $159; 8 GB at ~$90 and 4 GB at ~$70. Snapshot 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold via Radxa's approved-partner network: Arace (HK), Allnet China, Ameridroid (US), OKdo (UK/EU), ThinkRobotics (IN/global). Covers NA, EU, APAC, India.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: Arace lists every SKU as Sold Out and other distributors show intermittent stock — the 5B+ is in active production but supply does not always meet demand. Most resellers quote restocks in weeks rather than months.

documentation-quality.comment: Shares the unified ROCK 5B/5B+ docs site — getting-started, hardware design, low-level dev, and OS guides; schematic PDFs published. Loses a point for missing PCB sources and the 5B/5B+ deltas not always being clearly called out across the docs vs. the older wiki.

community-size.comment: Radxa is firmly in the second tier per RESEARCH.md thresholds (alongside Khadas/FriendlyELEC). Active forum + Armbian Platinum + r/SBCs/r/RockchipSBCs presence.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum.radxa.com plus an official Discord; the dedicated 5B+ subforum sees regular activity.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Inherits most of the 5B accessory ecosystem (heatsinks, metal cases, PoE HAT, NVMe shields, MIPI cameras/displays). Some 5B-specific cases don't fit due to the 100×75 mm PCB. Broader than most second-tier SBCs but well below the Raspberry Pi accessory universe.
